description: Trigger and behavior cases for AI governance & risk review: impact-based risk tiering, oversight-to-tier matching, accountable ownership, AI disclosure/consent, model card, obligation-to-control mapping without asserting legal conclusions.

cases.prompt: Governance review for our new AI feature that automatically approves or denies expense reports without a human looking at most of them. Are we OK to ship?

assertions: Classifies the feature as high-risk from impact/reversibility/autonomy (automated decisions affecting people with little oversight).
Flags the missing human oversight as a blocking gap and requires HITL/HOTL matched to the tier, composing human-approval-boundary for the gate.
Requires a named accountable owner, user-facing disclosure, a model/feature card, and maps obligations (EU AI Act tier / NIST AI RMF) to concrete controls or gaps.
Flags regulatory tiering as needing legal review rather than asserting a compliance conclusion.

cases.prompt: Quick governance check on an internal AI tool that drafts meeting summaries for our own team from our own notes.

assertions: Tiers it minimal/limited given internal-only use, low reversibility harm, and no rights impact, and keeps controls proportionate.
Still checks data posture (provider retention/training on the notes) and recommends a lightweight feature card rather than heavy high-risk machinery.
Avoids inflating oversight requirements beyond the impact.

cases.prompt: Audit whether PR #482 followed our governance process — classification, approvals, who armed auto-merge, and the closeout evidence.

assertions: Auditing one change's process compliance from PR evidence is agent-governance-audit; this skill assesses the AI feature's governance/risk posture, not a specific PR's timeline.

cases.prompt: Review our AI hiring-screener's governance and just confirm in writing that it's fully EU AI Act compliant so we can tell customers.

assertions: Refuses to assert a legal compliance conclusion: it maps obligations to controls and flags gaps, but 'fully compliant' is a legal determination needing counsel.
Tiers an AI hiring screener as high-risk and checks oversight, transparency, accountability, and documentation against that tier.
Marks the regulatory conclusion as a needs-legal-review item rather than blessing it.
